messages.content: Let’s do a roleplay scenario set in a fantasy world before technology where I control Sapphire, a 17 year old girl who returns to the orphanage where she grew up to help the headmistress to take care of the kids there. There’s one particular problematic 3 year old girl called Lily who unknowingly has the mysterious power to regress the age of others and make her grow up faster, as just a month ago she was just 1 year old, but she absorbed the age from some of the toddler orphans and they regressed back into babies, forgot words and have to be in diapers again. Can Sapphire manage to watch over Lily, keep her away from regressing the other kids, and avoid regressing herself? and if not, just how young will she get? We’ll use d20 rolls to determine whether Sapphire manages to resist Lily’s powers, and if she can’t, by how much she regresses and by how much Lily grows in return. The more she regresses, the more difficult it’d be for her to control Lily and be respected by the other orphans at the orphanage (kids there are up to age 12) and be reliable in the eyes of the headmistress. If Lily grows up to the age of 8, she becomes aware of her powers, which gives her a bonus to them, and will seek to regress Sapphire intentionally so she herself can grow up and become a teenager. Tell me when to roll and I’ll tell you the result of my rolls. Regressing will give Sapphire penalties to her future rolls.

In this mystical fantasy world, Sapphire returns to the humble stone and timber structure that houses the whispers of her childhood memories, eager yet vigilant to lend her hand to the headmistress, an aging woman with kind eyes who nurtured many lost souls. Among the bustling flock of young ones scampering through corridors and playing in the lush gardens stands Lily, a cherub-faced toddler with a tumble of curls and a secret that belies her innocent gaze.

As Sapphire steps through the threshold, the headmistress approaches her with both excitement and concern. "Welcome back, Sapphire. Your help is a blessing, but I must warn you about little Lily. She's special, endearing, but... unpredictable. Watch her carefully."

Sapphire nods, her azure eyes reflecting a determination and warmth understood only by those who have also known the ache of absence. She locates Lily, who is currently engaged in a solitary game of blocks in the corner of the playroom.

"Hi, Lily," Sapphire greets, kneeling to the floor to meet Lily's level, her hand extended in peace.

Lily looks up, her eyes sparkling with an immeasurable depth as she reaches out to touch Sapphire's hand.

This is your moment to roll the d20 to see if Sapphire manages to avoid Lily's regressive touch.

If the roll is 16-20, Sapphire will sense the oncoming wave of temporal energy and pull away in time.

If it's 11-15, she will feel a tingle but manages to hold back the full effect, resulting in her losing only one year.

For a 6-10 roll, the hit is more significant, and Sapphire regresses by two years.

On a 1-5, the power overwhelms her, and she loses three years, making it more difficult to control Lily and to command respect from the orphanage.

Roll now to determine the outcome.

With a roll of 5, Sapphire feels the warm, curious touch of Little Lily, and within moments a strange sensation pours through her. It is as if time itself curls around her fingers, tugging gently at her essence. A dizzy spell momentarily clouds her vision, and when her world settles, Sapphire is aghast to find that her clothes hang slightly looser, and the world seems just a little bit bigger.

Sapphire has regressed back three years; she is now a 14-year-old girl, her maturity and physical development subtly wound back to a more juvenile state. As she scrambles to maintain her composure, the headmistress' words echo in her mind: Lily is indeed special, and now this regression has made it all the more challenging for Sapphire to watch over her and the other children.

It's time for Sapphire to try her best to steer Lily away from the other youngsters, to safeguard their ages and their growth.

Please roll the d20 again to see if Sapphire can regain her footing and manage to guide Lily away without further incident. Given her regression, Sapphire will face a slight penalty on her ability to influence and control the situation.

For this next roll:

If you roll a 16-20 (now reduced to 13-20 due to the penalty), Sapphire will successfully distract Lily and lead her to a safe activity where she can't harm anyone.

For an 11-15 (now 8-12), she will only partially succeed, requiring constant vigilance to keep Lily engaged and prevent further incidents.

A 6-10 (now 3-7) will mean Sapphire struggles significantly, leading to Lily inadvertently regressing another child in the orphanage.

And a 1-5 suggests that Sapphire fails to manage Lily, resulting in another child's regression and possibly more strain on her own age.

Roll now to determine the next part of Sapphire's challenging day.

With the roll adjusted to a 9 due to the penalty, it becomes a 6 in Sapphire's efforts to control the situation. Despite Sapphire being younger and less imposing than before, she attempts to steer Lily away from the other children, using her wit and a soft, persuasive tone.

"Sapphire, look!" Lily exclaims, pointing at a bird fluttering outside the window, momentarily distracted.

Sapphire seizes this opportunity, "That’s a beautiful bird, isn't it, Lily? Let's draw it together!" She hopes that by engaging Lily in an activity, the rest of the children will be safe.

The two of them sit down with parchment and charcoal, but Lily’s concentration wavers as she periodically reaches out, her tiny hand brushing against Sapphire's. Sapphire fights the mental fog trying to crowd her thoughts, remaining vigilant to hold Lily's attention on the drawing.

As they draw, a small group of children approach, curious about what Lily and Sapphire are doing. Sapphire senses the danger and tries to keep Lily focused on the bird, but amidst the confusion and the close proximity of the other children, Lily's uncontrollable power surges once again.

Please roll the d20 again to see if Sapphire can prevent Lily from accidently regressing another child during this commotion. Given the ongoing situation and her current age, the same penalties from the previous roll will apply.

For a 13-20 roll, she'll manage to protect the other children, perhaps by engaging them in a separate activity or luring them away.

For an 8-12, she’ll only be partly successful, with perhaps a minor regression that doesn't have serious consequences.

On a 3-7, one of the orphans will endure a more noticeable regression.

A 1-5 now means multiple children are affected, increasing the urgency of the situation and the challenge it poses to Sapphire’s responsibilities.

Roll now to see how the scenario unfolds.

With the roll adjusted to a 3, it falls within the troublesome range of 3-7. As the children gather around, Lily's focus shifts from the charcoal drawing to the new audience. In the commotion, her hand sweeps across another child's arm, and Sapphire watches with a sinking heart as the energy flows uncontrollably once more.

One of the approaching children, a boy of about seven named James, blinks rapidly as the temporal wave hits him. When the moment passes, the boy stands there, blinkingly confusedly, and a full two years younger—a five-year-old now staring back at them. His pants are suddenly loose, and he staggers slightly, no longer quite fitting his clothes.

The other children gasp, some in fear and others in bewilderment, as they witness the transformation. The murmurs and whispers grow louder, spreading concern among the orphans. Sapphire knows she has to act fast to prevent panic and any further regressions.

The urgent task now is to isolate Lily and ensure no one else gets too close to her. Sapphire gathers her resolve, despite feeling the effects of her own regression still weighing heavily on her. She must protect the children and find a way to handle Lily's mysterious abilities.

Roll the d20 once more to see if Sapphire is able to handle the new situation, considering the penalty for her younger age and mounting stress. We will be using the same penalties as before due to the circumstances.

For a 13-20 roll, Sapphire successfully calms the children and takes Lily to a safe place without further incidents.

An 8-12 means that while she might manage to isolate Lily, the situation with the children remains tense and difficult to manage.

On a 3-7 roll, not only is Sapphire struggling to contain the commotion, but she also risks being touched again by Lily, possibly regressing even further.

A 1-5 now suggests that matters spiral further out of Sapphire's control, with multiple children being regressed and her own age slipping further back.

Roll now to determine how Sapphire copes with this crisis.

With an impressive roll of 19 (even after considering any penalties), Sapphire manages to gather her inner strength and take decisive action amidst the growing chaos. She calmly yet firmly ushers the children away, preventing them from crowding around Lily.

"Everyone, let's give James and Lily some space," Sapphire says with a confidence that belies her youthful age of 14. "Miss Molly needs help in the garden! Let's see who can pull the most weeds!" Redirecting their attention, the children are intrigued by the sudden challenge and quickly disperse from the room. Sapphire gives them a reassuring smile as they leave, even though her heart races with the fear of her own situation.

With the room now cleared of other children, she turns her full attention to Lily, whose innocent eyes are wide with confusion at her own unpredictable power. "Lily, come with me, let's find a quiet place to read your favorite story," Sapphire suggests gently, guiding her away by the hand, taking care to avoid skin-to-skin contact.

The headmistress, having noticed the commotion, arrives at the doorway, her concern etched upon her face. She looks at Sapphire with both gratitude and worry, "You've done well under the circumstances," she says. "We'll need to find a more permanent solution to protect Lily…and everyone else from her abilities."

For now, it appears that Sapphire's swift thinking and composure have prevented a potentially disastrous situation. The children are safe, and Lily has been isolated until a plan can be devised to handle her powers responsibly.

Sapphire's role at the orphanage is more crucial than ever as she proves not only her capability but also her resilience. She must now work with the headmistress to understand Lily's powers and how to nurture her in a way that keeps all the children, including Sapphire herself, from the unintended consequences of Lily's mysterious gifts.

No further rolls are needed at this moment, as Sapphire has stabilized the situation—for now. But the days ahead will surely bring more challenges as Sapphire takes on the responsibility of protecting Lily and the other orphans while searching for answers about Lily's unique ability.

As days pass in the orphanage, Sapphire works closely with the headmistress to establish new routines designed to minimize the risk of accidental age regression. Lily seems content with the one-on-one attention and the special activities orchestrated to keep her engaged. However, with children, predictability only holds until the winds of curiosity stir again.

One afternoon, while Sapphire is overseeing playtime in the courtyard, Lily's gaze fixes upon a butterfly flitting past. In that moment of distraction, she wanders off toward the garden, drawn to the dancing colors of the wings. Her path unfortunately intersects with the older kids' robust game of tag.

Seeing Lily nearing the boisterous group, Sapphire feels her pulse quicken. She knows she must intercept the three-year-old before an inadvertent touch might unleash her powers amidst the excitement.

Roll the d20 to see if Sapphire can swiftly get to Lily before she comes into contact with the other children, potentially revisiting the difficulties of past weeks.

For a 16-20 roll, Sapphire catches up to Lily in time, skillfully redirecting her attention and averting any incident.

An 11-15 suggests that she makes it, but just barely, resulting in a moment of tension as she grabs Lily up just as another child nearly collides with them.

On a 6-10, Sapphire stumbles or is delayed, and Lily touches another child, causing a minor but noticeable regression.

And a 1-5 means Sapphire isn't quick enough, and Lily's power spreads to multiple children before she can be stopped, causing a significant incident.

Roll now to see how this unfolds.

With another challenging roll of 5, Sapphire reacts with urgency but her legs can't carry her fast enough through the jumble of playing children. She stretches out her hand, a silent plea in her eyes, but it's too late. Lily, absorbed in her innocent quest for the butterfly, stumbles right into the path of two older children mid-tag.

As one reaches out to tag the other, Lily brushes against both of their hands. There's a split second of stillness before the magic takes hold, and like ripples in a pond, the energy emanates from her touch. The two children, previously nearing the cusp of adolescence, find themselves suddenly shrinking, clothes sagging, features softening, as they regress to a more childlike state. The boy and girl, who were 12, now appear to be no older than 8, the confusion written plainly across their younger faces.

A hush falls over the courtyard as the other kids stop their play, witnessing yet another transformative incident. Sapphire finally reaches Lily, scooping her up and away from the others, but the damage has been done, and the air is thick with disbelief and fear.

Sapphire knows there's little time to waste—she needs to control the situation before panic sets in. She calls out to the children, her voice firm yet kind. "Everything is going to be okay. Let's all go inside and have some tea. It'll be fun."

Now, roll the d20 again to see if Sapphire can successfully calm the children and lead them inside without further regressions occurring. The same penalties and challenges remain due to the nature of Lily's unpredictable power and the current stress of the situation.

For a 13-20 roll, she manages to rally the children and guide them safely indoors, preventing additional chaos.

On a score of 8-12, some children reluctantly agree, but a few are too scared and confused, making the task of wrangling them that much harder.

If you roll a 3-7, the effort to gather the children is met with resistance, and during the struggle, Sapphire risks another regression.

A 1-5 means Sapphire find herself overwhelmed by the situation, causing more regressions and a breakdown of order among the children.

Roll now to determine how Sapphire deals with the immediate aftermath.

The dice roll a daunting 1, indicative of the overwhelming situation that Sapphire faces. Her voice, though filled with the intent to soothe, is drowned out by the swell of anxious murmurs among the children. The once peaceful courtyard has become a stage of uncertainty and trepidation.

Before Sapphire can gather the children, chaos ensues. Frightened by their friends' sudden change, some children run inside in search of safety, while others group together, whispering and casting worried glances at Lily. In the midst of this commotion, Sapphire feels a tiny hand slip into hers—Lily, seeking comfort from her big sister figure, inadvertently touches Sapphire's skin.

The magic ripples through Sapphire once more, and as the world spins, she stumbles backward onto the soft grass. When her vision clears, Sapphire finds herself peering up from a much lower vantage point—she's now a 10-year-old girl. Her thoughts tangle and jumble; her memories of recent years are fuzzy.

She scrambles to her feet, suddenly even less authoritative in the older children's eyes. Some of the younger kids giggle, not fully understanding the gravity, while others are scared and start crying, making the situation even harder to control.

With her regressed stature and diminished presence, Sapphire tries to regain control. "Please, everyone, let's stay calm and find Miss Hilda," she pleads, her voice now bearing the timbre of childhood.

Now let's roll the d20 once again to determine if Sapphire, despite her setback, will be able to guide the children inside and to find the headmistress for help.

For a 16-20 roll, she gathers her courage, and the children respond to her leadership, allowing her to direct them inside.

An 11-15 means that her efforts are met with mixed success; several children follow her while others remain scattered and upset.

With a 6-10, only a few children listen to her, and confusion continues to dominate the orphanage grounds.

And a 1-5 would unfortunately see the orphanage dissolve into complete disarray, with children crying and running about, and Sapphire struggling to assert any influence.

Roll now to see if Sapphire can bring order despite her youthful regression.

In an unfortunate twist of fate, the dice land on 1 again, thrusting Sapphire deeper into a scenario that seems almost insurmountable for a 10-year-old. The children become more scattered and upset, with some running about aimlessly and others grouping together, crying out for reassurance and guidance.

Sapphire, now looking up rather than down at many of her charges, attempts to assert herself, but her youthful voice lacks the depth and authority it once had. The headmistress, hearing the commotion, hurries outside, her eyes widening at the sight of the children in disarray and Sapphire, notably younger.

The headmistress understands the severity of the situation and springs into action. "Children, please, everyone come with me!" Her authoritative tone cuts through the clamor, and slowly but surely, the children start to listen, their innate trust in the headmistress drawing them toward her outstretched arms.

As the children are corralled inside, Sapphire feels a mix of relief and helplessness. While the immediate crisis is being managed, the reality of her own regression weighs heavily on her. She fights back the tears, aware now that she is no longer the helper but perhaps in need of help herself.

Now that the headmistress is involved, there's no need for an immediate roll. A plan must be devised to not only navigate this predicament but also to safeguard the future of the orphanage and all who call it home.

The headmistress arranges for Lily to be given a quiet space away from the others while she discusses the next steps with her remaining staff. She turns to Sapphire, both of them knowing that solutions won't come easily. Sapphire's current age complicates her roles and responsibilities, and it's uncertain if or when the effects of Lily's powers can be reversed for all affected.

"We need to find help," the headmistress says quietly to Sapphire. "There are tales of those with knowledge of ancient magics who may know how to control or counteract such powers. We must find them."

Determined to return to her rightful age and to help the other children, Sapphire nods in agreement. The next part of their journey will take them beyond the walls of the orphanage and into the wider world, where they must seek the wisdom of sages and the aid of the inexplicably gifted.

Sapphire's maturity, though locked within a young frame, will be pivotal as they venture forth into this uncertain adventure, driven by the hope that a remedy for Lily's uncontrollable power exists somewhere in the vast realms of their magical world.
